Spanish Gangster Disciples.description: The Spanish Gangster Disciples is a Latino street gang originating in South Chicago, founded by Rudy Rios in the early 1980s as an offshoot of the Gangster Disciples. It is part of the Folk Nation alliance and is involved in drug trafficking, assault, robbery, and murder. The gang uses symbols such as the Star of David and colors baby blue and black. It remains active in Chicago.

Diablos Motorcycle Club.description: Outlaw motorcycle club founded in San Bernardino, California in 1961 by John J. 'Cadillac Jack' Baltas. The club has chapters across the United States, with a significant presence in New England. Membership is by invitation only and restricted to white males. Law enforcement considers it a second-tier outlaw motorcycle gang.

Los Lobos.description: Ecuadorian criminal and terrorist organization specializing in drug trafficking and contract killing. Formed in 2020 as a splinter group of Los Choneros after the assassination of its leader. Operates primarily in Latacunga, Cuenca, Machala, and Pastaza province, with over 8,000 members. Involved in major prison riots and the assassination of presidential candidate Fernando Villavicencio.

Peckerwood.description: Peckerwood is a US racial epithet reclaimed by a white subculture associated with prison gangs and outlaw motorcycle clubs. The term originated in the 19th century as a slur against poor rural whites. In the later 20th century, it became a label for white supremacist prison gangs, often used for entry-level recruits of the Aryan Brotherhood. The subculture is most common in California, Texas, and the U.S. South and Southwest.

Rochester crime family.description: The Rochester crime family was an Italian American Mafia crime family based in Rochester, New York, founded in the 1950s. It was originally a crew of the Buffalo crime family before becoming independent under Frank Valenti. The family engaged in racketeering, loansharking, gambling, extortion, arson, and murder. It was active until approximately 1993.

Tijuana Cartel.description: Mexican drug trafficking organization based in Tijuana, Baja California, founded in 1989 by the Arellano Félix brothers after the fragmentation of the Guadalajara Cartel. It was one of the largest and most violent cartels in Mexico during the 1980s and 1990s, but has been weakened by arrests and deaths of its leaders. Since 2014, it has allied with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG), effectively merging into the Cártel de Tijuana Nueva Generación (CTNG).

Yellow Henry Gang.description: A 19th-century New Orleans street gang active during the early 1870s and late 1880s. Originally led by Turpo, then by 'Yellow' Henry Stewart after Turpo's arrest in 1877. The gang engaged in burglaries, armed robberies, and extortions, attracting prominent criminals. It disbanded after leader Frank Lyons was sentenced to life imprisonment in 1892.
